formulas.name: Fiscal Deficit
formulas.formula: Fiscal Deficit = Government Expenditure - Government Revenue
formulas.notes: It shows the difference between government's income and expenditure.

formulas.name: Revenue Deficit
formulas.formula: Revenue Deficit = Government Expenditure - Government Revenue (excluding capital account transactions)
formulas.variables: Government Expenditure, Government Revenue (excluding capital account transactions)
formulas.notes: It shows the difference between government's income and expenditure, excluding capital transactions.

formulas.name: Budgetary Surplus
formulas.formula: Budgetary Surplus = Government Revenue - Government Expenditure
formulas.notes: It is the difference between the government's income and expenditure, which is saved.

formulas.name: Fiscal Policy Multiplier
formulas.notes: It is used to measure the effect of government spending on aggregate demand.

formulas.name: Government Budget Balance
formulas.formula: Government Budget Balance = Government Revenue - Government Expenditure
formulas.notes: It shows the difference between government's income and expenditure.

formulas.name: Fiscal Policy Effect
formulas.variables: ΔY (Change in National Income), MPC (Marginal Propensity to Consume), ΔG (Change in Government Expenditure)
formulas.notes: It shows the effect of government spending on national income.

formulas.name: Public Debt
formulas.formula: Public Debt = Total Amount of Loans and Borrowings + Outstanding Debts
formulas.variables: Total Amount of Loans and Borrowings, Outstanding Debts
formulas.notes: It is the total amount of debt owed by the government to its lenders.
